raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding American Expresss handling of a disputed credit-card charge in which the bank mischaracterized my claim, failed to evaluate coercion and misrepresentation, and defaulted improperly to a merchants no-refund policy despite evidence that valid consent did not exist. 

I disputed a {$10000.00} charge from XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year> for a XXXX XXXX purchased at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX My dispute was not based on dissatisfaction with the product. It was based on coercive sales tactics, misrepresentation, and lack of meaningful disclosure at the point of consent.

I was kept seated in a sales chair for over an hour while multiple sales representatives rotated through the interaction. As my flight departure approached, urgency was deliberately escalated. My credit card was taken from me while I remained seated, I was not taken to a checkout counter, and I was rushed into signing electronically. I was not informed verbally or in writing that the purchase was final or non-refundable before signing, and I was not given a meaningful opportunity to review the terms.

The merchant later admitted in writing that some information may be unclear because of the rush and that there was sales representative misinformation. Despite this, American Express repeatedly reframed my dispute as dissatisfaction with cosmetics and relied primarily on the merchants assertion of a no-refund policy allegedly posted in a checkout area I was never brought to.

By defaulting to merchant policy rather than evaluating whether valid consent existed, American Express failed to properly investigate a billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act.
